name: Swahili
register: Reverent contemporary Swahili in the register of the Swahili Union Version (Biblia, SUV 1952) — the most-trusted and liturgically dominant translation across East Africa — with the warmth of Neno: Biblia Takatifu where address to the Father is intimate. Dignified but personal, the tone in which a congregation prays 'Baba yetu'.
quote_convention: Double quotation marks "…" for direct speech, matching the Swahili Union Version; nested speech uses single marks '…'.
poetry_convention: Poetic lines (Psalms, prophetic oracles, canticles) are set as separate indented lines, one poetic colon per line, with parallel cola aligned as in the SUV Zaburi layout.

glossary.note: Builds on the beloved 'Baba yetu' of the Lord's Prayer; 'wa mbinguni' is the SUV phrasing for heavenly — filial, never 'Mungu'.
Primary filial divine name — the exact form East African believers pray daily ('Baba yetu uliye mbinguni'). NEVER revert to Mungu or BWANA.
Definite filial name; unmodified 'Baba' is understood as the Father from context, as in SUV John/1 John. Not 'Mungu Baba' (theological label), which cools the intimacy.
Same filial family as 'Baba yetu wa mbinguni' minus the possessive, for generic/vocative use.
Retains the Aramaic 'Aba' with the Swahili gloss 'Baba', exactly as SUV renders Rom 8:15 / Gal 4:6 — the child's cry preserved.
REVERSION TOKEN — the generic deity word. Non-filial contrast term; use only where English keeps 'God' as God-as-God, never for 'our Father'.
REVERSION TOKEN — SUV renders YHWH as BWANA in caps. Distinct from 'Bwana' (Lord/Jesus). Use only for the covenant name; never as a substitute for 'our Father'.
Standard SUV term, universally recognized.
Relational genitive ('of our Father'), not identity — keeps the filial vision without collapsing into 'Roho wa Mungu'.

glossary.gloss: the Son of Man / Son of Adam
glossary.note: Fixed SUV Christological title for Jesus' self-designation.
Unmodified 'Mwana' for the Son (Jesus) in Father–Son passages, mirroring SUV.
Traditional confessional title; retains 'Mungu' here because it is the fixed Christological formula, not a Father-address — reviewer to confirm vs 'Mwana wa Baba'.

glossary.gloss: the one and only / only-begotten Son
glossary.note: SUV John 3:16 'Mwanawe pekee'; 'wa pekee' carries the 'one and only' uniqueness.

Anchored to Swahili Union Version (Mt 26:29) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Ufalme wa Baba yetu". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Standard Swahili form.
Standard SUV form.
Transliteration used in SUV for the Hebrew title, distinct from the Greek-derived 'Kristo'.
Standard SUV term (e.g. Agano la Kale / Agano Jipya).
Renders hesed as loyal covenant love; 'thabiti' = unwavering. Reviewer to confirm vs the SUV 'fadhili' (kindness) tradition.
SUV word for gut-level mercy (racham / splanchnizomai), warmer than 'rehema'.
Standard SUV term covering both righteousness and justice.
SUV John 3:16 phrasing.

glossary.gloss: to be born anew / born again
glossary.note: Contemporary rendering of John 3:3,7; SUV reads 'kuzaliwa mara ya pili' (born a second time) — reviewer to choose which the community's ear prefers.
